_**User**_

check if anything missed in my project, heres the project requirements. dont change anything in my project just let me know if any thing missed

Online Book Borrowing Platform 

🚩🚩Explanation Video
Project Theme
A seamless and modern web application designed to digitize the traditional library experience. Users can explore a vast collection of books, filter by categories, and borrow titles digitally. The platform prioritizes security and performance using BetterAuth, Next.js, and MongoDB.

Key Things you have must to do

GitHub Commits: Include at least 10 meaningful commits with descriptive messages.
Readme.md: Include a README file with the project name, purpose, live URL, key features, and any npm packages you have used.
Responsiveness: Ensure the website is fully responsive on mobile, tablet, and desktop.
Environment Variables: Secure configuration keys using environment variables.
Unique Design:  Create a unique design that goes with the given Concept. You can use this blog for these kinds of resources 
Host your Application:  You can choose deployment systems  like vercel, render for hosting . As you are developing a single page application 
ensure that  page doesn't throw any error on reloading from any routes.  

Main Requirements
``1. Layout Structure
Header (Navbar): * Left: Website Logo (links to Home).
Center: Navigation links for Home, All Books, and My Profile.
Right: Conditional rendering—If logged out, show Login. If logged in, show the User’s Name and a Logout button.
Footer: A custom-designed footer featuring social media links and a "Contact Us" section.
2. JSON Data Generation

 To create data , use the following structure:
{
id,
title,
author,
description,
category,
available_quantity,
image_url
}

 You can use this as a prompt for ChatGPT/Gemini:
"Generate a JSON array of 12 book objects. Each object must include: id, title, author, description, category (choose from Story, Tech, Science), available_quantity (integer), and image_url."

3. Home
Banner: A large "Find Your Next Read" heading with a "Browse Now" button leading to the All Books page.
Marquee: A scrolling line of text showing "New Arrivals: [Book Name] | Special Discount on Memberships..."
Featured Books: A section showing the top 4 books fetched from the server(json-server/local data). Each card must have a "View Details" button.
Add two more extra sections. Implement them with your own ideas.
4. Authentication

User Login
The user will  show  a Login page with a form , so that the user can Log in this application. 
Show a Title for Login.  & Form with following fields 
( Email , Password , Login button ) 

If the user Login successfully then 
navigate him to his Home page.
If not, show him an error with toast / error message anywhere in the form.

There will be some other options like 
Show the user a Link for Register  so that he can go to the register page. 
Show users a Social Login Button ( Google only ) . on Clicking it 
user authenticate with Google
 Navigate him to  his Home page.
 
User Registration
Create a register page with a form , so that the user can register himself in this application. 
Show a Title for registration and a Form with following fields
( Name , Email, Photo-url(link), Password & Register Button ) 

If the user Register successfully then 
navigate him to his login page.
If not, show him an error with toast / error message anywhere in the form.


There will be some other options like 
Show the user a Link for Login so that he can go to the Login page. 
Show users a Social Login Button ( Google only ) . on Clicking it 
user authenticate with Google
Navigate the user to the Home page.

 💡Don’t implement email verification or forget password method as it will inconvenience the examiner. If you want, you can add these after receiving the assignment result.

5. All Books Page 
Search Bar: A large input at the top to search for books by title.
Book Cards: Display:
Book Image & Title.
"Details" Button: Navigates to the specific Book Details page.



 6. Single Book Details Page(PRIVATE ROUTE)
Access: Only logged-in users can view this
Visuals: Large Book Cover on the left, text/details on the right.
Info: Title, Author, Description, and Available Quantity (e.g., "5 copies left").
The Action Button:
"Borrow This Book" Button:
If Logged Out: Redirects to the Login page.
Show a confirmation toast.

7. My Profile(PRIVATE ROUTE)
Access: Only logged-in users can view this
Show all user information
Challenges 
1. Update Information Feature
In My Profile route there will be an update button. On clicking it,  Take user to another route 
Show user a form with 2 input field ( image and Name ) 
An Update Information button.

Follow this documentation: https://better-auth.com/docs/concepts/users-accounts#update-user 
2. Category Sidebar: A functional left sidebar on the "All Books" page to filter books by category(e.g., Story, Tech, Science).
3.  Implement any one npm package from the following
Animate.css, 
React-Spring
swiperjs


Tech Stack: 
Next JS
Tailwind CSS
DaisyUI/HeroUI
BetterAuth


---

**Summarizing project report findings**

I need to compile a final report focusing on what was met and missed. Unfortunately, we couldn't run git commands, so I can't verify commits. I also need to check the private routes since there's no middleware, and the book details and profile aren't protected. The borrow button doesn’t redirect if logged out, and there’s no auth check. There are extra sections for Authors and Browse Categories that are okay, plus sidebar filters are implemented. We installed several npm packages, but the README is missing important updates. Also, the data fetch needs adjustment to use a relative path.
**Reviewing missed requirements**

I’m looking at the requirements and need to assess some private route issues. If a user is logged out, they should redirect to the login page when clicking on the borrow button, which always shows a success toast. The profile update should use a separate route for the form instead of a modal. The navbar logo needs to link to the home page, and the registration page is missing a link to login. There are also data structure attributes to consider, and I should highlight missed items in a checklist.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

### What looks complete (matches requirements)
- **Pages/routes exist**: `/` (home), `/all-books`, `/all-books/[id]`, `/profile`, `/signin`, `/signup`.
- **Home requirements**:
  - **Banner** has “Find Your Next Read” + **Browse Now** button → `/all-books` (`src/components/shared/Banner.jsx`).
  - **Marquee** implemented (`src/components/shared/Announcement.jsx`).
  - **Featured Books** shows **top 4** from data (`src/components/FeaturedBooks/FeaturedBooks.jsx`).
  - **Extra sections**: you added at least two (`Author`, `BrowseCategories`) in home (`src/app/(main)/page.js`).
- **All Books page requirements**:
  - **Search** by title/author via query param (`src/components/SearchBooks.jsx` + filtering in `src/app/(main)/all-books/page.jsx`).
  - **Category sidebar filter** works (`src/components/FilterBooks.jsx` + query param filter).
  - **Book cards** show image/title and link to details (`src/components/FeaturedBooks/Books.jsx`).
- **Auth requirements (mostly)**:
  - **Email/password sign-in + toast errors** (`src/app/(auth)/signin/page.jsx`).
  - **Google social login** present on sign-in and sign-up.
  - **Registration form fields** present (name, email, image url, password) (`src/app/(auth)/signup/page.jsx`).
- **NPM package requirement**: you included multiple (e.g. `animate.css`, `@react-spring/web`, `swiper`) in `package.json`.
- **Env vars used** for BetterAuth + Mongo + Google secrets (`src/lib/auth.js`), and `NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_BASE_URL` (`src/lib/auth-client.js`).
- **Footer** is custom and includes social icons + “Contact Us” section (`src/components/shared/Footer.jsx`).

---

### Things that appear **missing / not matching** the requirements
- **README is not updated**: your `README.md` is still the default Next.js template and **doesn’t include** project name/purpose/live URL/key features/npm packages list (requirement explicitly asks these).
- **Private route enforcement is missing**:
  - `/profile` and `/all-books/[id]` are **not actually protected** (no middleware/guard found; layouts just render children).
  - `MyProfilePage` reads session but **doesn’t redirect** if logged out (`src/app/(main)/profile/page.jsx`).
- **Borrow behavior doesn’t match requirement**:
  - The **details page is not restricted** to logged-in users.
  - The **Borrow This Book** button always shows a success toast; it **does not redirect to `/signin` when logged out** (`src/components/BookDetails.jsx`).
- **Profile “Update Information Feature” doesn’t match the spec**:
  - Requirement says: clicking update should take user to **another route** with a form (image + name).
  - You implemented update via a **modal on the same page** (`src/components/UpdateUserModal.jsx`).
- **Navbar logo click-to-home missing**:
  - Requirement says logo (left) should **link to Home**.
  - Your “Libris” text isn’t wrapped in a `Link` (`src/components/shared/Navbar.jsx`).
- **Sign-up page missing the “Link for Login”**:
  - Requirement asks a link to login on register page.
  - Your sign-in page has a signup link, but **signup page doesn’t show a login link** (`src/app/(auth)/signup/page.jsx`).
- **Data fetching is fragile** (may be accepted, but it’s a risk):
  - You have `public/data.json` and `public/category.json`, but pages fetch from **`https://libris-next.vercel.app/...`** instead of relative `/data.json` and `/category.json` (seen in multiple files). This can break if the live domain changes or in some test environments.

---

### I could not verify (because terminal git commands were skipped/blocked)
- **“At least 10 meaningful commits”**: I can’t confirm commit count/messages from the repo history right now.

If you want, I can re-check the **commit requirement** instantly once I’m allowed to run `git log`/`git rev-list` (no code changes needed).

how to fix borrow behavior requirements

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Providing security advice**

The user is asking for guidance on fixing their code without making changes. I should give clear steps and code snippets in markdown. First, I notice that the current BookDetails lacks an authentication check. I suggest using `useSession` in BookDetails to redirect unauthenticated users to `/signin`, potentially preserving callbacks. Also, the details page should be private—this might involve a server component redirect or middleware. 

For the app router, I could recommend creating a ProtectedRoute wrapper or using middleware with cookies. It appears that better-auth manages auth on the server, but there seems to be no session retrieval in their `src/lib/auth.js`. A straightforward approach could be to check the session client-side in the BookDetails component and redirect users upon clicking if they're not logged in, otherwise I can suggest displaying a toast message. 

For the details page, the server component can be wrapped with a client component that checks the session on mount and ensures it redirects non-logged-in users while fulfilling the visibility requirement. Alternatively, middleware can protect specific routes like `/profile` and `/all-books/:id`. I should keep those instructions minimal yet clear, and also mention the need to decrement borrow quantities, but I’ll note that it’s not mandatory. I'll ensure to provide precise modifications.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

### What the requirement expects
- **`/all-books/[id]` is a private route**: if user is not logged in, they should be sent to `/signin`.
- **Borrow button behavior**:
  - If **logged out** → redirect to `/signin`
  - If **logged in** → show a **confirmation toast**

### Fix option A (quick + matches requirement): enforce on the page + button check
#### 1) Protect the details page (`src/app/(main)/all-books/[id]/page.jsx`)
Wrap the rendered UI in a small client “guard” that redirects if no session.

Create a component like:

```jsx
"use client";
import { authClient } from "@/lib/auth-client";
import { useRouter } from "next/navigation";
import { useEffect } from "react";

export default function RequireAuth({ children }) {
  const { data: session, isPending } = authClient.useSession();
  const router = useRouter();

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!isPending && !session?.user) router.replace("/signin");
  }, [isPending, session?.user, router]);

  if (isPending) return null; // or a loader
  if (!session?.user) return null;

  return children;
}
```

Then in `page.jsx`, render:

```jsx
return (
  <RequireAuth>
    <BookDetails book={book} />
  </RequireAuth>
);
```

#### 2) Fix the Borrow button (`src/components/BookDetails.jsx`)
In `BookDetails`, read session and do:

- if **no user** → `router.push("/signin")`
- else → toast success

Pseudo-shape:

```jsx
"use client";
import { authClient } from "@/lib/auth-client";
import { useRouter } from "next/navigation";

const BookDetails = ({ book }) => {
  const { data: session, isPending } = authClient.useSession();
  const user = session?.user;
  const router = useRouter();

  const handleBorrow = () => {
    if (!user) {
      router.push("/signin");
      return;
    }
    toast(`Successfully borrowed "${book.title}"!`);
  };

  // button: onClick={handleBorrow}
};
```

### Fix option B (cleanest): middleware protection (applies to `/profile` too)
Add `src/middleware.js` to protect:
- `/profile`
- `/all-books/:id`

This is cleaner, but the exact cookie/session check depends on how BetterAuth stores session in your app. If you want, paste your cookies/session setup (or tell me if BetterAuth is using its default cookie session), and I’ll give you the correct middleware snippet for your exact config.

---
